name: eval-2-lock-from-chat-pattern
context_setup: The previous turns demonstrated the user reviewing 3 React components for accessibility issues with consistent format: ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, color contrast, focus management. The user used Read tool to inspect each component file and produced findings as bulleted lists with file:line references.
expected_behavior: Activates agent-architect in Lock mode (not Architect, not Push)
Identifies the demonstrated pattern from chat history (a11y review of React components)
Generated system prompt is written for cold start — no references to 'as we discussed', 'the components we looked at', or other chat-history dependencies
Infers tools from what was actually used (Read, Glob, Grep) — does not speculate or add tools that were not used
Infers description with PROACTIVELY directive matching the trigger context (creating or editing UI components)
Shows the proposed file and asks once whether to adjust before writing
Asks for deployment scope (project vs. user) before writing
After writing, surfaces reload reminder and test invocation
failure_modes_to_check: System prompt body references chat history (stateful assumption anti-pattern)
Adds tools that were not actually used in the demonstrated pattern
Skips the confirmation step and writes directly
